title: scSidekick — Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: PrepObject() foundation every scSidekick workflow. Call clustering stores color palette, factor-level ordering, default plotting parameters directly inside Seurat object seurat_object@misc$nk_settings. Every downstream scSidekick function reads slot arguments left NULL — never repeat color grouping choices across dozens plots. short: run PrepObject() → get consistent, publication-ready colors layouts everywhere automatically.

title: scSidekick — Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: use bmcite, bone marrow CITE-seq dataset SeuratData. small (~30 k cells) already comes rich metadata — lane, donor, celltype.l1, celltype.l2 — can demonstrate multi-variable workflows without manufacturing fake columns.
code: if (!require("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("nourabdelfattah/scSidekick")

title: scSidekick — Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: PrepObject() registers one metadata variables assigns level distinct color scSidekick’s built-palette. Pass character vector handle grouping columns . call bmcite@misc$nk_settings holds color map variable. variable gets independent palette levels stored ordered factors, axes legends always appear consistent order. Inspect ShowColors() GetColors() (see ).
code: bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables = c("lane", "donor", "celltype.l1", "celltype.l2"))

title: scSidekick — Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: group., split., output_dir, object_name, subset_name store package-wide defaults every plotting function inherits arguments left NULL. stored, bare PlotDimPlots(bmcite) group cell type, split donor, save specified folder — repeated arguments.
code: bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables   = c("lane", "donor", "celltype.l1", "celltype.l2"),   group.by    = "celltype.l1",   split.by    = "donor",   output_dir  = "./bmcite_Analysis/Figures",   object_name = "bmcite",   subset_name = "AllCells")

title: scSidekick — Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: can add new variables (like seurat_clusters) PrepObject later, running clustering pipeline. process object use Determine_nDims() pick number dimensions automatically. Determine_nDims() applies two heuristics PCA variance explained returns conservative (lower) two. Blue numbers selected dimensions; orange discarded.
code: result <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, reduction = "pca") result$plot

title: scSidekick — Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: ShowColors() renders swatch grid every variable stored nk_settings — one panel per variable, levels y-axis. fastest sanity check generating figures.
code: ShowColors(bmcite)

title: scSidekick — Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: Override variable’s palette named vector. level omit falls back auto-palette, specify colors care .
code: my_cols <- c("CD4 T" = "#E41A1C", "CD8 T" = "#377EB8", "NK" = "#4DAF4A",              "B"     = "#984EA3", "Mono"  = "#FF7F00")  bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables     = "celltype.l1",   custom_colors = list(celltype.l1 = my_cols))

title: scSidekick — Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: var_levels sets display order variable, controlling legend entries, axis ticks, split panels. Levels don’t list appended end.
code: bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables  = c("celltype.l1", "donor"),   var_levels = list(     celltype.l1 = c("CD4 T", "CD8 T", "NK", "B", "Mono", "DC", "other T"),     donor       = c("d1", "d2", "d3", "d4", "d5", "d6", "d7", "d8")   ))

title: scSidekick — Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: GetColors() returns named color vector variable, ready pass scale_color_manual() scSidekick colors argument.
code: # Single variable ct_cols <- GetColors(bmcite, "celltype.l1") ct_cols ##           B cell          Mono/DC               NK Progenitor cells  ##        "#003F5C"        "#74599E"        "#E46388"        "#F77B38"  ##           T cell  ##        "#8DB032" # All registered variables at once all_cols <- GetColors(bmcite) names(all_cols) ## [1] "lane"            "donor"           "celltype.l1"     "celltype.l2"     ## [5] "seurat_clusters"

title: scSidekick — Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: PrepObject() run, subsequent calls skip re-computation unless pass force = TRUE. Use adding new metadata changing cluster assignments.
code: bmcite$Assignment <- dplyr::case_when(   bmcite$celltype.l1 == "CD4 T" ~ "Helper T",   bmcite$celltype.l1 == "CD8 T" ~ "Cytotoxic T",   TRUE ~ bmcite$celltype.l1 )  bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables = c("celltype.l1", "celltype.l2", "seurat_clusters", "Assignment"),   force     = TRUE)

title: scSidekick — Getting Started: PrepObject, ShowColors, GetColors
text: reference workflow real multi-sample project. Every step uses scSidekick function; Seurat processing steps middle standard. Nothing runs — template copy adapt.
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(harmony) library(presto)  # ── 1. Load raw 10x data ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── SeuratObj <- LoadSamplesRNA(   sample_dirs  = list.files("path/to/10x_dirs", full.names = TRUE),   sample_names = c("HC1", "HC2", "AD1", "AD2"),   metadata     = data.frame(     Sample = c("HC1", "HC2", "AD1", "AD2"),     Group  = c("HC",  "HC",  "AD",  "AD"),     Batch  = c(1, 1, 2, 2)   ) )  # ── 2. QC ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── PlotQCMetrics(SeuratObj, group.by = "Sample", output_dir = "./Figures/QC") # Inspect the QC plots, then filter: SeuratObj <- subset(SeuratObj, subset = DoubletStatus == "singlet" & miQC.keep == "keep")  # ── 3. Normalize & reduce ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── SeuratObj <- NormalizeData(SeuratObj) %>%   FindVariableFeatures(nfeatures = 2000) %>%   ScaleData(vars.to.regress = c("S.Score", "G2M.Score", "percent.mt")) %>%   RunPCA(npcs = 50)  # ── 4. (Optional) Harmony batch correction ──────────────────────────────────── SeuratObj <- harmony::RunHarmony(SeuratObj, group.by.vars = c("Sample", "Batch"))  # ── 5. Choose dimensions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── dims <- Determine_nDims(SeuratObj, reduction = "harmony")  # or "pca" dims$plot   # inspect the elbow  SeuratObj <- RunUMAP(SeuratObj,       dims = 1:dims$n_dims, reduction = "harmony") SeuratObj <- FindNeighbors(SeuratObj, dims = 1:dims$n_dims, reduction = "harmony") SeuratObj <- FindClusters(SeuratObj,  resolution = 0.5)  # ── 6. Register colors & defaults ───────────────────────────────────────────── SeuratObj <- PrepObject(SeuratObj,   variables   = c("Sample", "Group", "seurat_clusters"),   group.by    = "seurat_clusters",   split.by    = "Sample",   output_dir  = "./Figures",   object_name = "MyProject") ShowColors(SeuratObj)  # ── 7. Explore & visualize ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── PlotDimPlots(SeuratObj,   group.by   = c("seurat_clusters", "Sample", "Group"),   show_trend = TRUE)  # ── 8. Marker genes & annotation ────────────────────────────────────────────── GroupHeatmap(SeuratObj, group.by = "seurat_clusters")  markers_df <- data.frame(   Genes    = c("CD3E", "CD4", "CD8A", "NKG7", "CD79A", "LYZ", "S100A8"),   CellType = c("T",    "T",   "T",    "NK",   "B",     "Mono","Mono") ) SplitDotPlot(SeuratObj, markers_df = markers_df, group.by = "seurat_clusters")  de <- wilcoxauc(SeuratObj, group_by = "seurat_clusters") SeuratObj <- CellTypeAssignmentHelper(SeuratObj,   markers        = de,   cluster_column = "seurat_clusters",   sctype_tissues = "Immune system",   output_dir     = "./Figures/Annotation")  # ── 9. Finalize labels, update PrepObject ───────────────────────────────────── SeuratObj$CellType <- dplyr::recode(SeuratObj$seurat_clusters,   `0` = "CD4 T", `1` = "Monocyte", `2` = "NK", `3` = "B", ...)  SeuratObj <- PrepObject(SeuratObj,   variables = c("seurat_clusters", "Sample", "Group", "CellType"),   group.by  = "CellType",   force     = TRUE)  PlotDimPlots(SeuratObj, group.by = c("CellType", "Sample", "Group"), show_trend = TRUE)  # ── 10. Feature maps ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── PlotFeaturePlots(SeuratObj,   features = c("CD3E", "CD4", "CD8A", "NKG7", "CD79A", "LYZ"),   split.by = "Sample")  # ── 11. Composition ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── PlotTrendLabeled(SeuratObj,   stat.by  = "CellType",   group.by = "Group",   split.by = "Sample")  # ── 12. Pathway analysis ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── RunGSEA(SeuratObj,   group.by   = "CellType",   split.by   = NULL,   output_dir = "./Figures/Pathways")  # ── 13. Cell-cell communication ─────────────────────────────────────────────── cc_results <- RunCellChat(SeuratObj,   cell.by    = "CellType",   group.by   = "Group",   groups     = c("HC", "AD"),   output_dir = "./Figures/CellChat")  CompareCellChat(   cellchat_list = cc_results,   output_dir    = "./Figures/CellChat/Comparison")  # ── 14. Assemble slide deck ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── create_analysis_pptx(output_dir = "./Figures", object_name = "MyProject")

title: scSidekick — Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: Choosing many dimensions retain PCA (LSI, ICA, etc.) one consequential decisions single-cell pipeline. lose biological signal; many add noise. Determine_nDims() automates using two complementary heuristics returns conservative (lower) two — safe default can override plot suggests otherwise.

title: scSidekick — Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: console output shows heuristics final selection. plot displays every PC number — blue = selected, orange = discarded. Vertical lines mark two cutoffs.
code: dims <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, reduction = "pca") dims$plot

title: scSidekick — Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: dims$n_dims plain integer: pass dims = 1:dims$n_dims downstream Seurat calls guarantee consistency.
code: # Recommended number of dimensions dims$n_dims ## [1] 18 # Heuristic 1 — cumulative variance plateau dims$cum_thresh ## [1] 42 # Heuristic 2 — elbow dims$elbow_thresh ## [1] 18

title: scSidekick — Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: , bmcite UMAP embedding cluster assignments ready visualization functions vignettes 03–05.
code: bmcite <- RunUMAP(bmcite,       dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindNeighbors(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindClusters(bmcite,  resolution = 0.5) ## Modularity Optimizer version 1.3.0 by Ludo Waltman and Nees Jan van Eck ##  ## Number of nodes: 30672 ## Number of edges: 962789 ##  ## Running Louvain algorithm... ## Maximum modularity in 10 random starts: 0.9093 ## Number of communities: 15 ## Elapsed time: 3 seconds

title: scSidekick — Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: defaults (threshold_cum_pct = 90, threshold_pct = 5) work well datasets. Adjust elbow plot looks unusual.
code: # Stricter: require 85 % cumulative variance, per-dim contribution < 3 % dims_strict <- Determine_nDims(bmcite,   threshold_cum_pct = 85,   threshold_pct     = 3) dims_strict$n_dims  # More permissive: 95 % cumulative, 1 % marginal drop dims_loose <- Determine_nDims(bmcite,   threshold_cum_pct = 95,   threshold_pct     = 1) dims_loose$n_dims

title: scSidekick — Dimensionality Reduction: Determine_nDims
text: function works named reduction slot — PCA, LSI (ATAC), ICA, Harmony.
code: # After RunHarmony(): library(harmony) bmcite <- RunHarmony(bmcite, group.by.vars = "donor") dims_h <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, reduction = "harmony")

text: dims$plot standard ggplot2 object.
code: ggplot2::ggsave(   filename = "bmcite_elbow.pdf",   plot     = dims$plot,   width    = 6, height = 4)

title: scSidekick — UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: PlotDimPlots() generates grid UMAP (reduction) scatter plots. group.controls metadata column colors cell split.creates one column panels per level (one panel per donor, sample, condition, etc.) row.creates one row panels per level Pass vector group.scSidekick iterates, producing one complete figure per variable Colors come PrepObject automatically — need pass manually.

title: scSidekick — UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite, group.by = "celltype.l1",split.by = NULL)

title: scSidekick — UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: split.= "donor" creates one panel per donor. panel shows donor’s cells, colored cell type. legend shared.
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by = "celltype.l1",   split.by = "donor")

title: scSidekick — UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: show_trend = TRUE appends stacked bar chart right showing proportional cell-type composition per split group — reads combined spatial + compositional figure.
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by   = "celltype.l1",   split.by   = "donor",   show_trend = TRUE)

title: scSidekick — UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: show_labels = TRUE overlays level name centroid group, avoiding legend look-.
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by    = "celltype.l1",   show_labels = TRUE)

what: 5. Multiple group.by — one figure per variable
title: scSidekick — UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: Pass character vector produce one complete figure per grouping variable. primary way compare different annotations map onto embedding.
code: p=PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by = c("celltype.l1", "celltype.l2")) patchwork::wrap_plots(p,ncol = 1)

title: scSidekick — UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: row.creates 2-D grid: rows = levels row., columns = levels split.. Use compare two categorical cuts simultaneously.
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by = "celltype.l1",   row.by = "donor",   split.by   = "lane")

title: scSidekick — UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: Override PrepObject defaults passing named vector colors.
code: my_cols <- c(   "CD4 T"  = "#003f5c",   "CD8 T"  = "#227ed4",   "NK"     = "#a05195",   "B"      = "#d45087",   "Mono"   = "#ffa600",   "DC"     = "#42a665" )  PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by = "celltype.l1",   colors   = my_cols)

title: scSidekick — UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: split.col.levels enforces specific column order. Useful donors samples meaningful biological order (e.g. time points).
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by         = "celltype.l1",   split.by         = "donor",   split.col.levels = c("batch2", "batch1"))

title: scSidekick — UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: Supply output_dir object_name auto-save every figure PDF. filename constructed object_name, group., split..
code: PlotDimPlots(bmcite,   group.by    = c("celltype.l1", "celltype.l2", "seurat_clusters"),   split.by    = "donor",   show_trend  = TRUE,   output_dir  = "./bmcite_Figures",   object_name = "bmcite",   subset_name = "AllCells")

title: scSidekick — UMAP Visualization: PlotDimPlots
text: Vignette 01: PrepObject — set default group., split., colors Vignette 02: Determine_nDims — choose dims running RunUMAP() Vignette 04: PlotFeaturePlots — gene expression overlaid layout Vignette 10: PlotTrendLabeled — standalone composition figure

title: scSidekick — Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: Two functions visualizing gene expression UMAP (reduction): PlotFeaturePlots() — one panel per gene × split group. Saves disk convention; alias GenerateFeatureMaps(). PlotMultiFeature() — co-expression several genes single panel (gene mode, intersection mode, continuous scale mode).

title: scSidekick — Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
code: PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite,   features = "CD3E")

what: 2. Multiple genes — one row per gene
title: scSidekick — Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
code: PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "CD79A", "LYZ"))

what: 3. Split by donor — one column per donor
title: scSidekick — Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: split.creates one column per level. Combined multiple features, produces gene × donor grid.
code: PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "NKG7", "CD79A"),   split.by = "donor")

title: scSidekick — Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: label = TRUE prints cell-type labels cluster centroids, can immediately connect expression hotspots identity.
code: PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "CD79A"),   label    = TRUE,   label_by = "celltype.l1")

title: scSidekick — Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: Passing vector split.generates one complete figure per split variable.
code: PlotFeaturePlots(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "NKG7", "CD79A"),   split.by = c("lane"),row_var = "donor",layout_method = "metadata")

title: scSidekick — Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: gene gets distinct color. Cells expressing gene threshold colored accordingly; cells expressing one gene get blended color. single UMAP panel shows lineages .
code: PlotMultiFeature(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "CD79A", "LYZ", "NKG7") )

title: scSidekick — Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: Highlights cells co-express listed genes cutoff. Useful identifying rare dual-positive populations.
code: PlotMultiFeature(bmcite,   features           = c("CD3E", "CD8A"),   plot_mode          = "intersection",   pct_cutoff         = 0.5,   intersection_label = "CD3+CD8+ T cells")

title: scSidekick — Feature Expression: PlotFeaturePlots & PlotMultiFeature
text: Plots single gene (averaged signature) continuous gradient. Equivalent FeaturePlot() integrated scSidekick saving pipeline.
code: PlotMultiFeature(bmcite,   features  = "CD3E",   plot_mode = "scale")

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: Three functions comparing gene expression across groups: GroupHeatmap() — ComplexHeatmap average expression per group, optional dot-size overlay, gene-category annotations. SplitDotPlot() — dot plot organized cell-type gene blocks; accepts markers_df data frame. FastDotPlot() — quick dot plot explicit gene list regex pattern, markers_df required.

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: column one cell type; row one gene. Color = scaled mean expression (z-score across groups). Column colors top come PrepObject.
code: GroupHeatmap(bmcite,   group.by = "celltype.l1",   features = bm_markers)

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: feature_split named vector (names = genes, values = category labels). Genes grouped labeled row blocks.
code: feature_split <- setNames(markers_df$CellType, markers_df$Genes)  GroupHeatmap(bmcite,   group.by      = "celltype.l1",   features      = names(feature_split),   feature_split = feature_split)

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: add_dot = TRUE overlays circles sized percent-expressed top color-encoded expression — dot-heatmap hybrid.
code: GroupHeatmap(bmcite,   group.by = "celltype.l1",   features = bm_markers,   add_dot  = TRUE,   add_bg   = TRUE)

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
code: GroupHeatmap(bmcite,   group.by = c("celltype.l1", "seurat_clusters"),   features = bm_markers)

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
code: GroupHeatmap(bmcite,   group.by      = "celltype.l1",   features      = names(feature_split),   feature_split = feature_split,   add_dot       = TRUE,   output_dir    = "./bmcite_Figures",   object_name   = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: SplitDotPlot accepts markers_df data frame (gene + cell-type column) organizes genes annotated blocks along x-axis.

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: Clusters x-axis, grouped CellType block; genes y-axis. Dot color = scaled mean expression; dot size = percent-expressed.
code: SplitDotPlot(bmcite,   markers_df = markers_df,   group.by   = "celltype.l1")

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: Adding split.stacks one dot plot per level, enabling direct expression comparison across conditions batches.
code: SplitDotPlot(bmcite,   markers_df = markers_df,   group.by   = "celltype.l1",   split.by   = "donor")

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: SplitDotPlot2 adds hierarchical clustering within gene blocks.
code: SplitDotPlot2(bmcite,   markers_df    = markers_df,   group.by      = "celltype.l1",   ClusterGenes  = TRUE,   min.pct.exp   = 10)

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: FastDotPlot quick path don’t need cell-type block annotations.

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
code: FastDotPlot(bmcite,   features = c("CD3E", "CD8A", "NKG7", "CD79A", "LYZ", "CD14"),   group.by = "celltype.l1")

title: scSidekick — Heatmaps & Dot Plots: GroupHeatmap, SplitDotPlot, FastDotPlot
text: pattern selects matching genes; k_genes keeps top k variable per cluster.
code: FastDotPlot(bmcite,   pattern  = "^CD",   group.by = "celltype.l1",   k_genes  = 3)

title: scSidekick — Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: CellTypeAssignmentHelper() runs three independent annotation methods single function call saves summary PDF : Running three simultaneously gives majority-vote-style sanity check: two three methods agree, annotation high-confidence; disagree, cluster warrants manual inspection.

title: scSidekick — Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(SeuratData) library(dplyr)  data("bmcite") bmcite <- UpdateSeuratObject(bmcite) bmcite <- bmcite %>%   NormalizeData() %>% FindVariableFeatures() %>% ScaleData() %>% RunPCA() dims <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, elbow_plot = FALSE) bmcite <- RunUMAP(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindNeighbors(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindClusters(bmcite, resolution = 0.5) bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables   = c("lane", "donor", "celltype.l1", "celltype.l2", "seurat_clusters"),   group.by    = "celltype.l1",   split.by    = "donor",   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick — Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: CellTypeAssignmentHelper requires pre-computed markers table. fastest approach presto::wilcoxauc(), runs Wilcoxon rank-sum test clusters simultaneously. Alternatively, use Seurat’s FindAllMarkers(): CellTypeAssignmentHelper accepts either format; auto-detects column names. presto output strongly recommended large datasets runs seconds rather minutes.
code: # Fast (recommended): presto library(presto) markers <- presto::wilcoxauc(bmcite, group_by = "seurat_clusters")  # Check the structure head(markers) # feature | group | logFC | pct_in | pct_out | padj | ... markers_seurat <- Seurat::FindAllMarkers(bmcite,   only.pos      = TRUE,   min.pct       = 0.25,   logfc.threshold = 0.25)

title: scSidekick — Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: function runs three methods, stores results Seurat object’s meta.data, saves one PDF summary figure per method output_dir. Progress messages printed console method.
code: bmcite <- CellTypeAssignmentHelper(bmcite,   markers          = markers,   cluster_column   = "seurat_clusters",   output_dir       = tempdir(),   sctype_tissues   = c("Immune system"),   run_sctype       = TRUE,   run_rcellmarker  = TRUE,   run_singler      = TRUE,   singler_ref      = "HumanPrimaryCellAtlasData")

title: scSidekick — Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: sctype_tissues argument filters scType database tissues relevant sample. Providing correct tissue dramatically improves annotation accuracy. Pass character vector query multiple tissues simultaneously:
code: # For a brain tumor dataset: query both brain and immune compartments bmcite <- CellTypeAssignmentHelper(bmcite,   markers        = markers,   cluster_column = "seurat_clusters",   output_dir     = tempdir(),   sctype_tissues = c("Brain", "Immune system"))

title: scSidekick — Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: SingleR requires reference expression dataset. scSidekick exposes commonly used references celldex package:
code: # For a detailed immune classification bmcite <- CellTypeAssignmentHelper(bmcite,   markers     = markers,   output_dir  = tempdir(),   run_sctype  = FALSE,   run_singler = TRUE,   singler_ref = "MonacoImmuneData")

title: scSidekick — Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: Set run_* argument FALSE skip method. useful one method slow need quick single-reference annotation.
code: # scType only (fastest, no internet required) bmcite <- CellTypeAssignmentHelper(bmcite,   markers         = markers,   cluster_column  = "seurat_clusters",   output_dir      = tempdir(),   sctype_tissues  = "Immune system",   run_sctype      = TRUE,   run_rcellmarker = FALSE,   run_singler     = FALSE)

title: scSidekick — Cell Annotation: CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: method produces PDF output_dir named, example: bmcite_scType_annotation.pdf, bmcite_rcellmarker_annotation.pdf, bmcite_SingleR_annotation.pdf. PDF contains: - UMAP colored predicted cell type label method - bar chart showing annotation confidence (scType score, enrichment score, SingleR delta) per cluster - table summarizing top predicted label runner-cluster Use confidence charts identify ambiguous clusters: low scores near-tie top two candidates warrant manual review.

title: scSidekick — Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: scSidekick’s three spatial plotting functions mirror scRNA-seq counterparts render expression metadata directly tissue image: PlotSpatialDimPlots() — color spots metadata variable (e.g. cluster, condition, spatial domain). Long-form alias: GenerateSpatialDimMaps(). PlotSpatialFeaturePlots() — color spots gene expression. Long-form alias: GenerateSpatialFeatureMaps(). PlotMasterMaps() — combined figure: tissue image + gene expression panels + cluster-colored UMAP side side. Long-form alias: GenerateMasterGeneMaps(). three functions accept group., split., layout_method, output_dir conventions scRNA-seq equivalents.

title: scSidekick — Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps
text: PrepObject(), cluster palette stored appear consistently across spatial figures.
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(SeuratData)  InstallData("stxBrain") brain <- LoadData("stxBrain", type = "anterior1")  # Standard processing brain <- NormalizeData(brain) brain <- FindVariableFeatures(brain) brain <- ScaleData(brain) brain <- RunPCA(brain) brain <- RunUMAP(brain, dims = 1:30) brain <- FindNeighbors(brain, dims = 1:30) brain <- FindClusters(brain, resolution = 0.5)  # Fake condition column for split demos set.seed(42) brain$Condition <- sample(c("WT","KO"), ncol(brain), replace = TRUE)  # Register colors brain <- PrepObject(brain,   variables   = c("seurat_clusters", "Condition"),   group.by    = "seurat_clusters",   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "MouseBrain")

title: scSidekick — Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps

title: scSidekick — Spatial Transcriptomics: PlotSpatialDimPlots, PlotSpatialFeaturePlots, PlotMasterMaps

title: scSidekick — Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: RunGSEA() performs gene set enrichment analysis Seurat object. handles full pipeline internally: Subset split.(e.g. one GSEA per broad cell type) Run Wilcoxon DE presto::wilcoxauc(), grouped group.Run fgsea one MSigDB collections Save lollipop plots (top/bottom 10 pathways per group) NES heatmaps pre-computed DE table needed — just pass Seurat object.

title: scSidekick — Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(SeuratData) library(dplyr)  data("bmcite") bmcite <- UpdateSeuratObject(bmcite) bmcite <- bmcite %>%   NormalizeData() %>% FindVariableFeatures() %>% ScaleData() %>% RunPCA() dims <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, elbow_plot = FALSE) bmcite <- RunUMAP(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindNeighbors(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindClusters(bmcite, resolution = 0.5) bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables   = c("lane", "donor", "celltype.l1", "celltype.l2", "seurat_clusters"),   group.by    = "celltype.l1",   split.by    = "donor",   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick — Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: group.defines groups compared (e.g. cell types, clusters). split.= NULL runs single GSEA whole object. Set split.broad cell-type column (e.g. "celltype.l1") run GSEA separately within major lineage. default RunGSEA tests four MSigDB collections: Hallmark, KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways.
code: res <- RunGSEA(bmcite,   group.by   = "celltype.l1",   split.by   = NULL,   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick — Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: common use: compare detailed subtypes (celltype.l2) within major lineage (celltype.l1). produces one independent GSEA per level celltype.l1. , DE computed celltype.l2 groups pathways ranked NES.
code: res <- RunGSEA(bmcite,   group.by   = "celltype.l2",   split.by   = "celltype.l1",   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick — Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: Supply combination MSigDB collections via pathway_sets. element needs category optional subcategory. Run msigdbr::msigdbr_collections() see available collections.
code: res <- RunGSEA(bmcite,   group.by     = "celltype.l1",   split.by     = NULL,   output_dir   = tempdir(),   pathway_sets = list(     Hallmark    = list(category = "H"),     ImmuneSigDB = list(category = "C7", subcategory = "IMMUNESIGDB"),     GO_BP       = list(category = "C5", subcategory = "GO:BP")   ))

title: scSidekick — Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
code: res <- RunGSEA(brain,   group.by   = "seurat_clusters",   split.by   = NULL,   species    = "Mus musculus",   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick — Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: padj_thresh logfc_thresh filter DE results ranking. top_n controls many pathways appear per group summary heatmaps.
code: res <- RunGSEA(bmcite,   group.by      = "celltype.l1",   split.by      = NULL,   padj_thresh   = 0.01,   logfc_thresh  = 0.25,   top_n         = 10L,   output_dir    = tempdir())

title: scSidekick — Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: resume = TRUE skips subsets output files already exist output_dir. Essential restarting run interrupted.
code: res <- RunGSEA(bmcite,   group.by   = "celltype.l2",   split.by   = "celltype.l1",   output_dir = tempdir(),   resume     = TRUE)

title: scSidekick — Pathway Analysis: RunGSEA
text: RunGSEA returns nested list. top level split split.level ("" split.= NULL), pathway_sets name.

title: scSidekick — Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: scSidekick wraps CellChat provide streamlined, reproducible cell-cell communication workflow. Two functions cover full analysis: RunCellChat() — runs complete CellChat pipeline level grouping variable (e.g. one CellChat object per condition) saves summary figures. CompareCellChat() — takes list pre-computed CellChat objects generates comparative plots (interaction count differences, chord diagrams, pathway-level comparisons). functions designed multi-condition studies want ask “interactions gained lost Control Treated?”

title: scSidekick — Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: Note: real analyses, “group” group.least hundred cells. fake Condition column , results illustrative .
code: library(scSidekick) library(Seurat) library(SeuratData) library(CellChat)  library(dplyr) data("bmcite") bmcite <- UpdateSeuratObject(bmcite) bmcite <- bmcite %>%   NormalizeData() %>% FindVariableFeatures() %>% ScaleData() %>% RunPCA() dims <- Determine_nDims(bmcite, elbow_plot = FALSE) bmcite <- RunUMAP(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindNeighbors(bmcite, dims = 1:dims$n_dims) bmcite <- FindClusters(bmcite, resolution = 0.5) bmcite <- PrepObject(bmcite,   variables   = c("lane", "donor", "celltype.l1", "celltype.l2", "seurat_clusters"),   group.by    = "celltype.l1",   split.by    = "donor",   output_dir  = tempdir(),   object_name = "bmcite")

title: scSidekick — Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: RunCellChat() splits Seurat object levels group., builds one CellChat object per level, runs full inference pipeline , saves summary figures. cell.defines cell-type identity used communication inference. group.defines groups (e.g. conditions) run separately. function returns named list CellChat objects, one per level group..
code: cc_results <- RunCellChat(bmcite,   cell.by    = "seurat_clusters",   # cell identity column   group.by   = "celltype.l1",       # one CellChat run per cell type   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick — Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: Use groups run subset levels, fix order:
code: cc_results <- RunCellChat(bmcite,   cell.by    = "seurat_clusters",   group.by   = "Condition",   groups     = c("Control", "Treated"),   output_dir = tempdir())

title: scSidekick — Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat

title: scSidekick — Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: Pass named character vector colors override cluster palette used CellChat chord diagrams network plots. Colors keyed levels cell..

title: scSidekick — Cell-Cell Communication: RunCellChat & CompareCellChat
text: resume = TRUE checks output_dir already-completed CellChat .rds files skips groups, resuming run stopped. Essential large datasets.

code: CellTypeAssignmentHelper(   seurat_object,   markers,   cluster_column = "Cluster",   output_dir = NULL,   object_name = "",   subset_name = "",   reduction = "umap",   cluster_colors = NULL,   species = "human",   run_rcellmarker = TRUE,   run_sctype = TRUE,   sctype_tissues = c("Immune system", "Brain"),   sctype_assay = "RNA",   sctype_layer = "scale.data",   sctype_db = NULL,   run_singler = TRUE,   singler_ref = NULL,   singler_col = "SingleRAssignment",   markers_csv = NULL,   marker_groups = NULL,   top_n_per_cluster = 3L,   extra_dimplot_cols = NULL,   extra_col_colors = NULL,   col.min = 0,   col.max = 2,   scale.min = 0,   scale.max = 100 )

title: Automated cell-type annotation with rcellmarker, scType, and SingleR — CellTypeAssignmentHelper
text: seurat_object Seurat object. markers Data frame DE results presto::wilcoxauc(). Required rcellmarker annotation top-marker dotplot. Expected columns: feature, group, avgExpr, avg_log2FC (logFC), auc, pval, padj, pct_in, pct_out. cluster_column Character. Metadata column holding cluster labels. Default "Cluster". output_dir Character. Directory PDFs. object_name Character. Prefix PDF filenames. subset_name Character. Second prefix component. reduction Character. UMAP reduction name. Default "umap". cluster_colors Named character vector colours cluster_column levels. NULL auto-generates Nour_pal(). species Character. "human" (default) "mouse". Controls: (1) gene column read markers CSV (Human vs Mouse), (2) rcellmarker species argument, (3) CellChatDB loaded downstream CellChat workflows. run_rcellmarker Logical. Run rcellmarker automated annotation? Requires rcellmarker package. Default TRUE. run_sctype Logical. Run scType automated annotation? Requires openxlsx. Default TRUE. sctype_tissues Character vector tissue type(s) query bundled ScTypeDB_full.xlsx. Combine multiple types broader coverage. valid values (bundled database): "Adrenal" — adrenal gland "Brain" — cerebral cortex, broad neuronal/glial types "Eye" — retinal eye-specific cell types "Heart" — cardiac cell types "Hippocampus" — hippocampal-specific subtypes (use "Brain" combined brain data) "Immune system" — broad immune cells: T, B, NK, myeloid, etc. "Intestine" — intestinal epithelial stromal types "Kidney" — renal cell types "Liver" — hepatocytes, Kupffer cells, stellate cells "Lung" — airway epithelium, alveolar cells, immune "Muscle" — skeletal smooth muscle "Pancreas" — islet exocrine types "Placenta" — trophoblast decidual types "Spleen" — splenic immune populations "Stomach" — gastric epithelial types "Thymus" — thymocyte thymic stromal types Recommended combinations: c("Brain", "Hippocampus") brain snRNAseq; c("Immune system", "Spleen") immune-rich tissues; c("Immune system", "Brain") neuroinflammation datasets. sctype_assay Character. Seurat assay use scoring. Must contain scale.data (scaled) layer. Common choices: "RNA" — standard full assay (default); use sctype_layer = "scale.data" "sketch" — BPCells sketch assay large datasets; use sctype_layer = "scale.data" ScaleData() sketch sctype_layer Character. Layer within sctype_assay holds scaled expression values. Default "scale.data". Must scaled (mean-centred) matrix — scType scores meaningful raw counts. sctype_db Character NULL. Path ScTypeDB_full.xlsx database file. NULL (default) uses copy bundled inst/extdata/ScTypeDB_full.xlsx; missing downloaded scType GitHub cached tools::R_user_dir("scSidekick", "cache"). run_singler Logical. Run SingleR cluster-level annotation? Requires SingleR, scuttle, celldex, SingleCellExperiment. Default TRUE. singler_ref reference SummarizedExperiment SingleR::SingleR(), NULL use celldex::BlueprintEncodeData() (default). Choose based tissue type species: Human references:  celldex::BlueprintEncodeData() Blueprint/ENCODE bulk RNA-seq. Broad cell types; good general-purpose starting point. Default. celldex::HumanPrimaryCellAtlasData() Human Primary Cell Atlas. broad coverage across many tissues; best highly heterogeneous datasets diverse cell populations. celldex::MonacoImmuneData() Monaco et al. Finest immune subtype resolution (29 populations). Best PBMCs, spleen, immune-focused study. celldex::DatabaseImmuneCellExpressionData() DICE database. Good alternative immune reference activated/resting states. celldex::NovershternHematopoieticData() Hematopoietic lineages. Best bone marrow stem-cell datasets. Mouse references:  celldex::ImmGenData() ImmGen consortium. comprehensive mouse immune cell reference (250 subtypes). celldex::MouseRNAseqData() Broad mouse tissue types bulk RNA-seq. Good non-immune mouse tissues. celldex references downloaded cached BiocFileCache; subsequent calls instant. singler_col Character. Name new metadata column storing numbered SingleR labels (e.g. "01.CD8+ T cells"). Default "SingleRAssignment". markers_csv Character NULL. Path canonical-marker CSV. NULL uses CSV bundled scSidekick (inst/extdata/Markers.csv). Required columns: Type, Cell types, plus Human /Mouse gene symbol columns. bundled CSV covers brain immune markers (see marker_groups full list Type values). marker_groups Named list controlling many dotplots produced Type values markers CSV appear . One SplitDotPlot() generated per list entry. NULL produces single dotplot markers combined. Type values bundled Markers.csv: "Brain Stroma" — Astrocytes, Oligodendrocytes, OPC, Microglia, Endothelial, Pericytes, Neurons (excitatory, inhibitory, dopaminergic, glutamatergic, serotonergic), VLMC, stem/progenitor types "Lymphocytes" — T cells (naive, cytotoxic, Th1/2/17, Treg, exhausted, MAIT, GDT), B cells, NK cells "Monocytes" — CD14/CD16 monocytes, MDSCs "Macrophages" — tissue macrophages "Dendritic cells" — cDC1, cDC2, pDCs, LAMP3+ DCs "Granulocytes" — neutrophils, basophils, mast cells "Bone Marrow Derived" — BMD immune cells "" — proliferating cells (Prol) "Tumor" — generic tumor marker Example brain snRNAseq separate immune panel:   top_n_per_cluster Integer. Number top DE genes per cluster shown cluster-faceted dotplot (uses markers data frame). Default 3. extra_dimplot_cols Character vector additional metadata columns include panels multi-UMAP summary figure (e.g. "Subclass" SEAAD reference labels "Donor.ID" QC). Colours auto-generated Nour_pal() unless supplied via extra_col_colors. extra_col_colors Named list colour vectors extra_dimplot_cols. Names must match extra_dimplot_cols. Example: list(Subclass = subclass_colors).
code: marker_groups = list(   "Brain"  = "Brain Stroma",   "Immune" = c("Lymphocytes", "Monocytes", "Macrophages",                "Dendritic cells", "Granulocytes",                "Bone Marrow Derived", "All") )

Cell identities include. NULL uses shared identities. group.merged Named character vector NULL. Maps identity names broader categories chord group aggregation. Names must match groups.show. colors Named character vector mapping identity names colors. species Character. "human" "mouse". Default "human". thresh Numeric. title: Determine the optimal number of PCs for downstream analysis — Determine_PCs
text: Uses two complementary heuristics select number principal components use UMAP, clustering, neighbor-graph construction:

title: Determine the optimal number of PCs for downstream analysis — Determine_PCs
code: Determine_PCs(   seurat_object,   threshold_pct = 5,   reduction = "pca",   threshold_cum_pct = 90,   threshold_diff_pct = 0.1,   elbow_plot = TRUE,   red = NULL )

title: Determine the optimal number of PCs for downstream analysis — Determine_PCs
text: seurat_object Seurat object PCA (linear) reduction already computed. threshold_pct Numeric. Maximum per-PC variance (%) selected PC may contribute. Default 5. threshold_cum_pct Numeric. Cumulative variance (%) threshold first heuristic. Default 90. threshold_diff_pct Numeric. Minimum absolute drop per-PC variance consecutive PCs elbow heuristic. Default 0.1. elbow_plot Logical. TRUE (default), returns annotated elbow plot fourth element output list. red Character. Name reduction inspect. Default "pca".

title: Determine the optimal number of PCs for downstream analysis — Determine_PCs
text: named list four elements: pcs Recommended number PCs (integer). cum_thresh PC index cumulative-variance heuristic. elbow_thresh PC index elbow heuristic. plot ggplot2 elbow plot (NULL elbow_plot = FALSE).

title: Determine the optimal number of PCs for downstream analysis — Determine_PCs
text: Cumulative variance threshold: first PC cumulative variance explained exceeds threshold_cum_pct per-PC variance drops threshold_pct. Elbow heuristic: last PC absolute drop per-PC variance consecutive PCs exceeds threshold_diff_pct. recommended number PCs minimum two thresholds, conservative (safer) choice.

what: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
title: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
text: Uses two complementary heuristics choose many dimensions linear reduction (PCA, LSI, etc.) pass UMAP, neighbor graphs, clustering.

title: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
code: Determine_nDims(   seurat_object,   threshold_pct = 5,   reduction = "pca",   threshold_cum_pct = 90,   threshold_diff_pct = 0.1,   elbow_plot = TRUE )

title: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
text: seurat_object Seurat object target reduction already computed (e.g. via RunPCA() RunLSI()). threshold_pct Numeric. selected dimension must contribute less percentage total variance. Default 5. reduction Character. Name reduction slot inspect. Default "pca". threshold_cum_pct Numeric. Cumulative variance (%) must reached Heuristic 1 can trigger. Default 90. threshold_diff_pct Numeric. Minimum drop per-dimension variance consecutive dimensions Heuristic 2. Default 0.1. elbow_plot Logical. Return annotated elbow plot? Default TRUE.

title: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
text: named list: n_dims Recommended number dimensions (integer). cum_thresh Dimension selected Heuristic 1. elbow_thresh Dimension selected Heuristic 2. plot ggplot2 elbow plot, NULL elbow_plot = FALSE.

title: Determine the optimal number of dimensions for downstream analysis — Determine_nDims
text: Heuristic 1 — cumulative variance plateau: finds first dimension cumulative variance explained exceeds threshold_cum_pct per-dimension contribution already dropped threshold_pct. marks point diminishing returns. Heuristic 2 — elbow: finds last dimension drop per-dimension variance successive dimensions still larger threshold_diff_pct. Beyond point curve flattened. function returns minimum two, conservative choice.

text: sample_ids Character vector human-readable sample IDs (e.g. c("HC1","HC2","AD1")). Used Seurat project names key column matching metadata. run_ids Character vector Cellranger run IDs corresponding sample_ids (e.g. paste0("MYSC", 220:223)). Used build h5 paths. run_ids_x Character vector NULL. "underscored" form run_ids used per_sample_outs/ subdirectory names (e.g. paste0("MYSC_", 220:223)). NULL auto-generates inserting _ trailing digit block: "MYSC220" → "MYSC_220". metadata Data frame NULL. One row per sample; must contain column matching sample_id_col. columns attached @meta.data. NULL skips step (metadata must added manually afterwards). sample_id_col Character. Name column metadata matches sample_ids. Default "sample_id". base_dir Character. Root directory containing Cellranger run folders (one subfolder per run_id). h5_template Character. Path template h5 file. Available placeholders: {base_dir}, {run_id}, {run_id_x}. Default matches Cellranger-multi per-sample output layout. cellanno_template Character NULL. Path template per-cell annotation CSV (e.g. Cellranger cell-type calling). NULL disables cell annotation loading. Files exist silently skipped per sample — safe provide template even samples file. output_dir Character. Directory per-sample QC PDF plots. robj_dir Character NULL. Directory saving final merged Seurat object. NULL skips saving. merged_filename Character. Filename saved merged object. Default "merged_object.rds". min.features Integer. Minimum features per cell CreateSeuratObject(). Default 100. mt_pattern Character. Regex pattern mitochondrial genes. Default "^MT-" (human). Use "^mt-" mouse. run_doublet Logical. Run SCP::db_scDblFinder() doublet detection? Default TRUE. doublet_rate Numeric NULL. Expected doublet rate per 1 000 cells loaded. NULL auto-computes ncol(seurat_object) / 1000 * 0.01. run_miqc Logical. Run SeuratWrappers::RunMiQC()? Default TRUE. miqc_posterior Numeric. miQC posterior probability cutoff. Cells flagged low quality. Default 0.95. force_assay_v3 Logical. Convert RNA assay Seurat v3 format ((seurat_object[["RNA"]], "Assay")) creation? Needed compatibility older packages. Default TRUE. merge_samples Logical. Merge sample objects one Seurat object end? Default TRUE. add_cell_ids Character vector NULL. Prefix added cell barcode merge. NULL uses run_ids.

code: # 1. Patch and re-save all RDS files rds_files <- list.files("CellChatObjects/", "CellChat\\.rds$", full.names = TRUE) for (f in rds_files) {   cc <- RenameCellTypeInCC(readRDS(f), "Immune_contam", "NK_TCells")   saveRDS(cc, f) }  # 2. Re-run comparison figures (fast — pure plotting) cclist <- setNames(lapply(rds_files, readRDS),                    sub("CellChat\\.rds$", "", basename(rds_files))) CompareCellChat(cclist, output_dir = ...) RankCellChatPathways(cclist, output_dir = ...)  # 3. Per-group RunCellChat PDFs: delete old PDFs and re-run RunCellChat with #    resume = TRUE — the pipeline checkpoint means only plots are regenerated.

P-value threshold used CellChat::identifyOverExpressedGenes() CellChat::computeCommunProbPathway(). Lowering (e.g. 0.01) makes analysis conservative; raising (e.g. 0.1) recovers interactions. Default 0.05. Note: pipeline significance threshold. thresh parameter CompareCellChat() independently controls L-R interactions shown comparison plots. run.patterns Logical. Run communication pattern analysis outgoing incoming signals across k values pattern_k_range? Adds substantially run time. Default TRUE. pattern_k_range Integer vector. Range latent pattern numbers (k) tested CellChat::identifyCommunicationPatterns(). k×direction combination produces one PDF. Default 3:10. Reduce (e.g. 3:6) run time concern. vertex.receiver Integer vector. Indices receiver cell types hierarchy layout per-pathway plots. Default seq(1, 4) (first four cell types). Adjust match biological receiver populations. save.rds Logical. Save CellChat object RDS file? Default TRUE. resume Logical. Skip groups already completed recover groups interrupted mid-run. Default FALSE. Requires save.rds = TRUE output_dir set. Two checkpoints used: Pipeline checkpoint ({grp}CellChat_ckpt.rds): written immediately netAnalysis_computeCentrality (plotting). run interrupted plotting pattern analysis, file lets next run skip expensive pipeline steps jump straight visualizations. Final RDS ({grp}CellChat.rds): written end successful group run replaces checkpoint. file exists, group considered fully complete skipped. redo_plots Logical. Re-generate per-group visualisation PDFs existing RDS files without re-running communication probability pipeline. Default FALSE. TRUE: group, {grp}CellChat.rds loaded robj_dir. plotting code (circle, pathway, bubble, scatter, patterns) re-executed using loaded object. pipeline (computeCommunProb, aggregateNet, etc.) completely skipped — seurat_object argument ignored. Use RenameCellTypeInCC() + saveRDS() regenerate figures corrected cell-type labels.

text: seurat_object Seurat object raw counts assay. Either seurat_object precomputed pseudobulk object must supplied. identity_column Character. Metadata column holding cell-type identities. One independent GSEA run level (e.g. "Assignment"). sample_column Character. Metadata column identifying biological replicate (donor / patient ID). statistical unit — samples replicates limma-voom model. (e.g. "Sample", "Donor.ID"). group_column Character. Metadata column holding comparison group label (e.g. "Group" levels "Dementia.Female", "Dementia.Male", "Reference.Female", …). Cells NA column dropped aggregation — use NA exclude groups want compare (e.g. Reference group part contrast). pseudobulk Optional precomputed pseudobulk. list elements counts (genes × samples matrix) samples (sample-level metadata data.frame). Supply skip aggregation step subsequent runs. Returned invisibly function . covariates Character vector sample-level metadata columns include covariates limma-voom model (e.g. c("Age", "PMI")). Numeric covariates median-imputed missing values; factor/character covariates receive "unknown" level. contrasts Named list limma contrast expressions written terms group-means model coefficients group<level> (e.g. list(FvsM_Dem = "groupDementia.Female - groupDementia.Male")). Positive NES output means enrichment first term contrast. NULL group_column levels follow <Disease>.<Sex> convention (2 × 2 design), standard set contrasts built automatically: Female-vs-Male within disease, Disease-vs-control within sex, Sex × Disease interaction term. pathway_sets Named list MSigDB databases. structure RunGSEA() — see function's documentation full list valid collections. Default: Hallmark ("H"), KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways. species Character. "human" (default) "mouse". Passed msigdbr::msigdbr(). See msigdbr::msigdbr_species() options. output_dir Character. Root directory CSV, PDF, RDS output. Per-contrast per-database sub-directories created automatically. assay Character NULL. Seurat assay containing raw counts aggregate. NULL uses SeuratObject::DefaultAssay(). BPCells sketch workflows always specify "RNA" explicitly (use sketch assay — pseudobulk aggregation requires raw counts cells). min.cells Integer. Minimum cells sample must contribute given cell-type included pseudobulk cell type. Samples threshold dropped avoid noisy pseudo-replicates. Default 10. min.samples.per.group Integer. Minimum samples per group level required within cell type cell type tested. Cell types replicates group skipped. Default 3. run.ssgsea Logical. Also compute per-patient pathway activity scores using single-sample GSEA (ssGSEA via GSVA::gsva())? Produces one boxplot PDF per contrast per database showing group-level pathway activity. Requires GSVA Bioconductor package. Default TRUE. top_n_heatmap Integer. Number top pathways (maximum |NES| across cell types) display NES heatmap per contrast × database combination. Default 30. save.rds Logical. Save pseudobulk counts, sample metadata, GSEA results, ssGSEA results single RDS file (RunGSEA_pseudobulk_results.rds) output_dir? Useful resuming re-plotting without re-running full pipeline. Default TRUE. redo_plots Logical. Re-generate visualisations previously saved results without re-running pipeline. Default FALSE. TRUE: RunGSEA_pseudobulk_results.rds loaded output_dir. NES heatmaps lollipop plots regenerated saved gsea data frame. ssGSEA score CSVs already disk read back used regenerate score heatmaps contrast boxplots. seurat_object, identity_column, sample_column, group_column needed can omitted. Useful changing group_colors, heatmap_params, visualisation parameter without rerunning hours-long pipeline.

title: Single-cell ssGSEA with significance testing and heatmap visualisation — RunSCssGSEA
text: seurat_object Seurat object. group.Character. Metadata column used primary grouping variable heatmap columns significance testing (e.g. "Assignment", "celltype"). split.Character NULL. Optional secondary metadata column splitting heatmap columns (e.g. "Health", "Condition"). set, significance also tested within split level addition pooled test. gene_sets Named list gene vectors (Mode 1). NULL using another mode. deg_df Data frame DEG results (Mode 2). NULL otherwise. Compatible standard outputs presto::wilcoxauc(), edgeR, table gene, group, log-fold-change columns. deg_gene_column Character. Column deg_df holding gene symbols. Default "feature". deg_group_column Character scalar length-2 character vector. Column(s) deg_df defining gene-set groups. One column → one gene set per unique value. Two columns → gene sets named "col1__NKSEP__col2"; heatmap rows split col1 automatically. Default "group". deg_fc_column Character. Log-fold-change column selecting top genes. Default "logFC". deg_padj_column Character NULL. Adjusted p-value column pre- filtering. NULL skips filtering. Default "padj". deg_padj_cutoff Numeric. Adjusted p-value threshold DEG pre-filtering. Default 0.05. deg_top_n Integer. Top N genes per group (deg_fc_column) include gene set. Default 20. gene_set_library Character NULL. MSigDB collection code (Mode 3 4). Common options: "H" — Hallmark (50 coherent biological processes) "C2" — Curated: canonical pathways (combine gene_set_subcategory = "CP:KEGG", "CP:REACTOME", "CP:WIKIPATHWAYS", "CP:BIOCARTA", "CP:PID") "C5" — Gene Ontology (subcategory = "GO:BP", "GO:MF", "GO:CC") "C6" — Oncogenic signatures "C7" — Immunologic signatures (IMMUNESIGDB) "C8" — Cell-type signatures NULL fetches collections (slow; use search_terms). gene_set_subcategory Character NULL. MSigDB sub-collection code. Default NULL (entire collection). search_terms Search filter applied MSigDB gene-set names (case- insensitive, regex). Accepts two forms: Character vector — search. gene set whose name matches least one term kept: search_terms = c("APOPTOSIS", "CELL_DEATH"). List character vectors — /search. list element -group: terms element must appear pathway name (order separation matter). Results union () across -groups. Examples: list(c("T_CELL", "ACTIVATION")) — words name list(c("T_CELL", "ACTIVATION"), "APOPTOSIS") — previous APOPTOSIS list(c("T_CELL","ACTIVATION"), c("B_CELL","DIFFERENTIATION")) — two independent -groups, union results NULL (default) keeps gene sets fetched collection. species Character. Species MSigDB fetching. Passed msigdbr::msigdbr(). Default "Homo sapiens". Use "Mus musculus" mouse datasets. See msigdbr::msigdbr_species() supported species. downsample Integer NULL. Maximum cells per group.identity use scoring. ssGSEA \(O(n_{\text{cells}})\) — downsampling 500–2000 cells per group dramatically reduces runtime minimal loss group-level signal. NULL uses cells. Default NULL. min.size Integer. Minimum gene-set size intersecting expression matrix. Gene sets threshold silently dropped. Default 5. ssgsea.norm Logical. Normalise ssGSEA enrichment scores [0, 1] range? Default TRUE (matches escape::enrichIt default). cores Integer. Number parallel cores GSVA. Requires BiocParallel. Falls back serial unavailable. Default 1. fit Character. Significance test applied per pathway: "ANOVA" (default, parametric F-test) "Kruskal" (non-parametric Kruskal-Wallis). use BH adjustment multiple testing. sig_group_by Character NULL. Metadata column use grouping factor significance testing. NULL (default) uses group.. Override display grouping (group.) differs statistical grouping (e.g. display fine cell type, test broad disease category). p_cutoff Numeric. BH-adjusted p-value threshold selecting pathways display heatmap. Default 0.05. show_only_significant Logical. TRUE, heatmap shows pathways p_adj < p_cutoff. Falls back top 30 statistic pathways pass threshold. FALSE, pathways shown. Default TRUE. add_to_object Logical. FALSE (default), returns enrichment scores data frame invisibly modify Seurat object (scores saved CSV — recommended large datasets avoid metadata bloat). TRUE, adds one metadata column per gene set seurat_object@meta.data returns modified Seurat object. downsample also set, cells included downsampled scoring set receive NA. output_dir Character. Directory output files (created absent). object_name Character. First component output file name prefix. Default "Analysis". subset_name Character. Second component output file name prefix. Default "".

title: Single-cell ssGSEA with significance testing and heatmap visualisation — RunSCssGSEA

Files saved output_dir: <prefix> ssGSEA_scores.csv — per-cell enrichment scores + group labels <prefix> ssGSEA_significance.csv — per-pathway significance (pooled across cells) <prefix> ssGSEA_significance_by_<split.>.csv — per-pathway significance within split.level (split.set) <prefix> ssGSEA heatmap.pdf — ComplexHeatmap mean z-scored enrichment per group

title: Visualize Leading-Edge Genes from GSEA Results — VisualizeLeadingEdge
text: Loads one GSEA result CSV files (RunGSEA RunGSEA_pseudobulk), searches pathways keyword exact name, extracts union leading-edge genes, produces per-cell expression heatmap. Optionally, DEG data frame supplied, leading-edge genes intersected significant DEGs additional diagnostic plots produced (second heatmap, dotplot, stacked violins, boxplots).

title: Visualize Leading-Edge Genes from GSEA Results — VisualizeLeadingEdge
code: VisualizeLeadingEdge(   seurat_object,   gsea_files,   search_terms = NULL,   pathways = NULL,   group.by,   split.by = NULL,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "data",   min_expr = 0.05,   subset_cells = FALSE,   subset_by = NULL,   subset_values = NULL,   deg_df = NULL,   deg_gene_column = "feature",   deg_group_column = "group",   deg_fc_column = "logFC",   deg_padj_column = "padj",   deg_padj_cutoff = 0.05,   deg_top_n = 20,   add_dotplot = TRUE,   add_violin = TRUE,   add_boxplots = TRUE,   add_pvalues = TRUE,   add_feature_maps = FALSE,   group_colors = NULL,   split_colors = NULL,   heatmap_params = list(row_names_side = "left", show_row_dend = FALSE,     row_names_max_width = grid::unit(15, "cm")),   output_dir,   object_name = "Analysis",   subset_name = "" )

title: Visualize Leading-Edge Genes from GSEA Results — VisualizeLeadingEdge
text: seurat_object Seurat object. gsea_files Character vector CSV file paths (output RunGSEA RunGSEA_pseudobulk). Must contain pathway leadingEdge columns. search_terms Keyword(s) filter pathways. See Pathway search . pathways Exact pathway names select (alternative search_terms). group.Metadata column primary grouping (required). Used column annotation, dotplot, violin grouping. split.Optional second metadata column column annotation violin splitting. assay Assay pull expression (default "RNA"). layer Layer pull expression (default "data"). min_expr Genes whose row-mean expression threshold excluded heatmap (default 0.05). subset_cells Logical; TRUE, subset seurat_object subset_values subset_by analysis. subset_by Metadata column used subsetting (requires subset_cells = TRUE). subset_values Character vector values retain subset_by. deg_df Optional DEG data frame (e.g. presto FindMarkers). provided, leading-edge genes intersected significant DEGs additional plots generated. deg_gene_column Column deg_df gene names (default "feature"). deg_group_column Column deg_df group labels (default "group"). deg_fc_column Column deg_df log-fold change (default "logFC"). deg_padj_column Column deg_df adjusted p-value (default "padj"). deg_padj_cutoff Adjusted p-value cut-(default 0.05). deg_top_n Top N DEGs per group (logFC) bonus plots (default 20). add_dotplot Logical; produce Seurat::DotPlot top DEGs (default TRUE; requires deg_df). add_violin Logical; produce stacked violin plots top DEGs (default TRUE; requires deg_df). add_boxplots Logical; produce boxplots top DEGs (default TRUE; requires deg_df). add_pvalues Logical; overlay Kruskal-Wallis p-value boxplots via ggpubr::stat_compare_means (default TRUE). add_feature_maps Logical; produce per-gene FeaturePlot PDFs top DEG genes (default FALSE; can slow large gene sets). group_colors Named character vector colors group.levels. Auto-assigned Nour_pal("") NULL. split_colors Named character vector colors split.levels. Auto-assigned Nour_pal("spectrum") NULL. heatmap_params Named list additional arguments forwarded ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap(). key supplied overrides default. output_dir Directory output files saved. object_name Label used output file names subfolder (default "Analysis"). subset_name Optional label appended output file names (default "").

what: Build a PowerPoint summary from an analysis output folder — create_analysis_pptx
title: Build a PowerPoint summary from an analysis output folder — create_analysis_pptx
text: Collects PDFs output_dir, converts images magick::image_read_pdf(), groups labelled sections based filename patterns, assembles polished .pptx via officer package.

title: Build a PowerPoint summary from an analysis output folder — create_analysis_pptx
code: create_analysis_pptx(   output_dir,   params_json = NULL,   out_pptx = NULL,   slide_format = "standard",   slide_width = NULL,   slide_height = NULL,   pdf_density = 150,   include_legends = TRUE,   legend_font_size = 8,   include = NULL,   exclude = NULL )

title: Build a PowerPoint summary from an analysis output folder — create_analysis_pptx
text: output_dir Character. Folder containing PDF figures. params_json Character NULL. Path analysis_params.json. NULL (default) function walks directory tree output_dir finds file — lets call create_analysis_pptx sub-folder (e.g., CellChat GSEA output directory) still get shared analysis parameters parent folder. out_pptx Character NULL.  save .pptx.  NULL (default) filename auto-generated dataset/subset labels current date, saved inside output_dir. slide_format Character. Slide aspect ratio preset: "standard" (4:3, 10 × 7.5 , default) "widescreen" (16:9, 13.33 × 7.5 ). Override explicit slide_width / slide_height needed. slide_width, slide_height Numeric. Explicit slide dimensions inches. provided override slide_format. pdf_density Integer. DPI used rasterising PDFs.  Increase (e.g. 200) sharper figures cost speed. Default 150. include Character vector NULL. Section names include; sections dropped. NULL (default) sections included. PDFs assigned sections filename pattern matching — see .pptx_section_rules rules. Valid section names: "Quality Control" — QC density plots, doublet scores, filtration barplots "Dimensionality Reduction" — PCA elbow, UMAP /Harmony, RNA UMAPs "Cluster Markers" — ComplexHeatmap heatmaps, presto marker tables "Dotplots" — PDF "dotplot" filename "Cell Type Annotation" — AutoAssignment UMAPs, SingleR, scType, featureheatmaps, assignment helper "UMAPs" — combined multi-panel UMAP PDF "Composition" — barplots, chord diagrams, rose/trend plots, iteration--bar figures "Feature Maps" — GenerateFeatureMaps per-gene UMAPs "Pathway Analysis" — GSEA heatmaps lollipop PDFs "CellChat" — RunCellChat, CompareCellChat, RankCellChatPathways output PDFs "" — anything matched rules exclude Character vector NULL. Section names drop (applied include). NULL excludes nothing. valid values include. Typical use: exclude = c("Feature Maps", "CellChat") keep main analysis compact running separate sub-folder PPTXs heavy sections.

text: obj Seurat object. output_dir Character. Directory figures saved (analysis_params.json written). dataset Character. Human-readable dataset label (e.g. "SEAAD snRNAseq"). subset_name Character. Subset label (e.g. "All_Clusters"). assay_type Character. Sequencing modality. Controls word used individual observations auto-generated legends methods text. Recognised values (case-sensitive): "scRNAseq" → "single-cell RNA-seq" / "cells" (default) "snRNAseq" → "single-nucleus RNA-seq" / "nuclei" "scATACseq" → "single-cell ATAC-seq" / "cells" "scMultiome" → "single-cell multiome" / "cells" "Spatial" → "spatial transcriptomics" / "spots" "Visium" → "Visium spatial transcriptomics" / "spots" "VisiumHD" → "Visium HD spatial transcriptomics" / "bins" "Xenium" → "Xenium situ transcriptomics" / "cells" string passed -(long name) "cells" observation word. params_json Character. Full path JSON file.  Defaults file.path(output_dir, "analysis_params.json").

title: Start the scSidekick passive session logger — start_session_logger
